notes: This JSON is an import-ready shape for PEPTQ Sheets (Catalog/CatalogBeta).
Only fields present in the PDF are populated; technical/commercial fields are left blank for operator fill-in.

entries.title: BPC-157
entries.product_title: Synthetic peptide fragment utilized in laboratory models to evaluate cellular signaling associated with vascular response, cell migration, and extracellular matrix interactions - 10 MG
entries.description: Synthetic peptide fragment derived from a gastric peptide sequence, utilized in controlled research environments to investigate cellular communication pathways associated with angiogenesis, cytoskeletal organization, and extracellular matrix signaling under defined experimental conditions.
entries.description_short: Synthetic peptide fragment derived from a gastric peptide sequence, utilized in controlled research environments to investigate cellular communication pathways associated with angiogenesis, cytoskeletal organization, and extracellular matrix signaling under defined experimental conditions.
key_features: High-purity peptide fragment for controlled signaling pathway research
Lyophilized for enhanced stability and precise laboratory handling
Analytical QC verified for identity, purity, and batch consistency
research_focus_non_clinical: Angiogenic signaling and vascular pathway modulation
Cellular migration dynamics and cytoskeletal reorganization
Extracellular matrix interaction and structural signaling pathways
entries.notes_for_investigators: Utilized in cellular and tissue-response models to evaluate signaling pathways involved in structural adaptation, vascular response, and intercellular communication dynamics.
entries.regulatory_use: For laboratory research use only. Not for human or veterinary use. No medical or dosing information provided.

entries.title: AOD-9604
entries.product_title: Peptide fragment modeled on the lipolytic domain of human growth hormone, utilized to evaluate adipocyte-specific signaling and metabolic pathway selectivity - 5 MG
entries.description: Peptide fragment derived from the C-terminal lipolytic region of human growth hormone (GH 176-191), utilized in controlled laboratory environments to investigate adipocyte metabolism, pathway selectivity, and signaling responses independent of full growth hormone receptor activation.
entries.description_short: Peptide fragment derived from the C-terminal lipolytic region of human growth hormone (GH 176-191), utilized in controlled laboratory environments to investigate adipocyte metabolism, pathway selectivity, and signaling responses independent of full growth hormone receptor activation.
key_features: High-purity peptide fragment for targeted metabolic pathway research
Lyophilized for enhanced stability and precise laboratory handling
Analytical QC verified for identity, purity, and batch consistency
research_focus_non_clinical: Adipocyte metabolism: lipolysis and fatty-acid oxidation signaling vs. storage pathways
Energy utilization dynamics and nutrient partitioning mechanisms
Pathway selectivity profiling relative to intact GH receptor signaling
entries.notes_for_investigators: Utilized in adipose-focused research models to isolate metabolic signaling pathways without broad GH-IGF axis activation, enabling targeted evaluation of lipid metabolism and pathway-specific responses.
entries.regulatory_use: For laboratory research use only. Not for human or veterinary use. No medical or dosing information provided.

entries.title: CJC-1295 (NO DAC)
entries.product_title: Short-acting GHRH analogue utilized in controlled laboratory models to evaluate pulsatile growth hormone signaling, receptor activation timing, and endocrine pathway modulation - 10 MG
entries.description: Synthetic analogue of growth hormone-releasing hormone (GHRH) designed for controlled research applications to investigate pulsatile GH secretion dynamics, receptor-level activation timing, and downstream endocrine signaling under physiologic-pattern stimulation conditions.

entries.regulatory_use: For laboratory research use only. Not for human or veterinary use. No medical or dosing information provided.

entries.title: IPAMORELIN
entries.product_title: Selective ghrelin-receptor agonist utilized in controlled laboratory models to evaluate targeted GH secretion signaling, receptor selectivity, and downstream metabolic pathway modulation - 10 MG
entries.description: Synthetic pentapeptide engineered to selectively activate ghrelin (GHS-R1a) receptors, utilized in controlled research settings to investigate targeted growth hormone release, receptor-level selectivity, and downstream metabolic and endocrine signaling pathways with minimized off-target activity.
